channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A top official with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party quits, a year before the national election
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A top official with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party announced his resignation on Monday, citing health problems, a year before an election in which the center-left Social Democrats face an uphill struggle, Kevin Kühnert, 35, has been the Social Democrats' general secretary — the
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The world's rivers faced the driest year in three decades in 2023, the UN weather agency says
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. weather agency is reporting that 2023 was the driest year in more than three decades for the world's rivers, as the record-hot year underpinned a drying up of water flows and contributed to prolonged droughts in some places.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Samoa warns of 'highly probable' fuel spill after New Zealand navy ship caught fire and sank
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A fuel spill is “highly probable” after a New Zealand navy ship grounded, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa, the Pacific island nation's acting prime minister said late Sunday. All 75 people on board the HMNZS Manawanui were taken to safety on life boats.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mozambique headed for crucial elections amid jihadist insurgency and drought-induced hunger
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambicans will vote this week for a new president who many hope will bring peace to an oil- and gas-rich northern province that has been ravaged by a jihadist insurgency for nearly seven years.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO and MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Pakistani separatist group claims bombing that killed 2 Chinese near Karachi airport
item.description: A Pakistani separatist group has claimed responsibility for a late night bombing that targeted a convoy with Chinese nationals outside Karachi's airport, killing two workers from China and wounding eight people.
item.dc:creator: Adil Jawad And Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US spends a record $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since last Oct. 7
item.description: The United States has spent a record of at least $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since the war in Gaza began and led to escalating conflict around the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Sao Paulo mayor's race heads to a runoff without a controversial outsider in Brazil's elections
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Incumbent Ricardo Nunes and leftist lawmaker Guilherme Boulos will face each other in a runoff to decide the next mayor of Sao Paulo, following a first round of voting Sunday in Brazil’s local elections.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris is pressed on support for Netanyahu in '60 Minutes' preview
item.description: Vice President Harris was pressed on the U.S.’s support for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in an upcoming interview on CBS News’s “60 Minutes.” “We supply Israel with billions of dollars in military aid,” correspondent Bill Whitaker said in a clip highlighted by Mediaite.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Israeli official warns 'everything is on the table' as IDF prepares response to Iranian missile attack
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Yoav Gallant told Fox News on Sunday that the IDF is preparing a response to Iran's missile attacks, and said that "everything is on the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bolton blasts Biden for publicly opposing possible Israeli strikes on Iran
item.description: Former White House national security adviser John Bolton sharply criticized the Biden administration Sunday for speaking publicly against the possibility of Israel striking nuclear sites or oil fields in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Schiff supports Israeli strike on Iranian ballistic missile factories
item.description: Rep. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.), the leading candidate for his state's Senate seat, said he would advise Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to strike Iranian ballistic missile factories.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel intensifies bombardment of northern Gaza, southern Beirut
item.description: Israel escalated its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Beirut amid concerns about an intensifying conflict in the region with Iran and its military proxy groups, including Hezbollah and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Pope names 21 new cardinals, significantly increasing the pool who will one day elect his successor
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis named 21 new cardinals Sunday, significantly increasing the size of the College of Cardinals and further cementing his mark on the group of prelates who will one day elect his successor.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's Macron calls for end to arms deliveries for use in Gaza
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Saturday called for a halt in deliveries of arms used in Gaza to Israel, adding that France was not sending arms to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Powerful new explosions rocked Beirut's southern suburbs late Saturday as Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon, also striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clinton Defense chief says Netanyahu 'does not have any confidence' with Biden
item.description: William Cohen, President Clinton’s former Secretary of Defense, hypothesized that Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has “no confidence” in his relationship with President Biden in a Saturday Fox News appearance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Former CIA head signals full-scale war inevitable if Israel 'goes all out'
item.description: Former Secretary of Defense and CIA Director Leon Panetta said the war in the Middle East is at a crucial point now and future actions will signal whether there will be an imminent “full scale war.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Rising from the ashes: Israelis in nation's war-torn south move home a year after October 7 massacre
item.description: The unique communal structure of the kibbutz plays a vital role in fostering resilience and support as people heal from the devastating impact of the October 7 massacre with many now returning home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Expert warns UN's role in AI regulation could lead to safety overreach
item.description: Nations have pursued a number of options to better globally coordinate artificial intelligence safety initiatives, including several safety summits and the creation of safety institutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli airstrikes rock southern suburbs of Beirut and cut off a key crossing into Syria
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel carried out another series of punishing airstrikes Friday, hitting suburban Beirut and cutting off the main border crossing between Lebanon and Syria for tens of thousands of people fleeing the Israeli bombardment of the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sanxingdui Ruins were discovered accidentally by a farmer; has since been the site of over 60K relics
item.description: Sanxingdui Ruins is an archaeological site in Guanghan City. Official archaeological excavations have been conducted since 1934 at the site after its discovery in 1929.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wildlife lover facing charges after allegedly assaulting passersby while attempting to capture a baby seagull
item.description: A Belfast woman was in court Tuesday after allegedly attacking passersby who tried to intervene with her capture of a baby seagull in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bus filled with wedding guests plunges into a ravine, killing 7
item.description: A speeding bus filled with wedding guests crashed in southeastern Pakistan on Thursday, killing seven of the passengers while injuring 17 others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yazidi woman held hostage for 10 years in Gaza rescued in Israel, US operation
item.description: A Yazidi woman held captive by a Hamas terrorist after she was abducted by ISIS a decade ago has been returned to her family after she escaped through coordinated efforts by Israeli and U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran warns of 'decisive response' if Israel crosses 'red lines'
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian warned this week at the Asia Cooperation Dialogue summit that Israel cannot act with "impunity" and "crossing [Iran's] red lines" will be met with a "decisive response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting near luxury Mexico resort leaves 1 dead, suspects flee on jet skis
item.description: A Mexican national was shot and killed Wednesday near a ritzy resort in Cancun before two suspects fled on jet skis, according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli troops battle Hezbollah in Lebanon as fears of a wider war mount after Iran's missile attack
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli troops battled Hezbollah militants inside Lebanon on Wednesday after launching what they say are limited ground operations there.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A US bomb from World War II explodes at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway
item.description: Officials said an investigation by the Self-Defense Forces and police confirmed that the explosion was caused by a 500-pound U.S. bomb and there was no further danger.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation
